summary.category: India's Geographical Location, Size, and Global Significance
points: India lies entirely in the Northern Hemisphere (latitude 8°4'N to 37°6'N, longitude 68°7'E to 97°25'E) with the Tropic of Cancer dividing it into tropical (south) and subtropical (north) zones; its 3.28 million square km land area makes it the seventh largest country (2.4% of world's land) yet supporting 17% of world population, bordered by 15,200 km land boundary and 7,516.6 km coastline including Andaman-Nicobar and Lakshadweep island territories.

India's central location between East and West Asia, with the Deccan Peninsula extending into the Indian Ocean (after which the ocean is named), provides unparalleled maritime strategic importance; the 82°30'E Standard Meridian (Mirzapur) maintains unified Indian Standard Time despite a two-hour east-west time difference, demonstrating political unity across geographical diversity.

Historically, India exchanged intellectual contributions (Upanishads, Ramayana, Panchtantra, numerals, decimal system) and commodities (spices, muslin) through ancient land routes and maritime paths, receiving architectural and cultural influences from Greek and West Asian sources; today, India shares land boundaries with Pakistan-Afghanistan (northwest), China-Nepal-Bhutan (north), Myanmar-Bangladesh (east), and maritime borders with Sri Lanka and Maldives (south), anchoring it as a central geopolitical power in South Asia.
